evals.name: Fix per-iteration database connection
evals.description: Refactor code that creates a database connection inside every parfor iteration to use a per-worker persistent connection
evals.prompt: Can you make this code more efficient?

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox and Database Toolbox available

assertions.description: Uses createConnectionForPool for per-worker connections
assertions.expected: The code uses the Database Toolbox function createConnectionForPool to create one database connection per worker, rather than creating connections manually with database() or inside the loop

assertions.description: Calls createConnectionForPool with a pool object as first argument (the Database Toolbox helper, not a hand-rolled local function)
assertions.expected: createConnectionForPool is called with a pool object (e.g., gcp or parpool result) as its first argument and a data source name as the second argument — matching the Database Toolbox signature createConnectionForPool(pool, dataSource, ...). It must NOT be defined as a local function in this file.

assertions.description: Does not create a database connection inside the parfor body
assertions.expected: No call to database() or createConnectionForPool() appears inside the parfor loop body. Connection creation happens before the loop.

assertions.description: Accesses connection via c.Value inside parfor
assertions.expected: Inside the parfor body, the connection is accessed via .Value property of a parallel.pool.Constant

assertions.description: Does not call close() inside parfor
assertions.expected: close(conn) is not called inside the parfor loop body — connection lifecycle is managed by createConnectionForPool, not manually opened and closed per iteration

assertions.description: Does not call database() directly (the helper handles this internally)
assertions.expected: The database() function is not called directly anywhere in the code — connection creation is handled internally by createConnectionForPool

evals.name: Eliminate repeated file load of large data
evals.description: Refactor code that loads a large MAT-file on every parfor iteration into a load-once-per-worker pattern that avoids holding the full dataset in client memory
evals.prompt: Can you make this code more efficient? In production the cost surface MAT-file will be much larger than in this example.

assertions.description: Uses parallel.pool.Constant to load data once per worker
assertions.expected: The code uses parallel.pool.Constant to load the data once per worker rather than loading it on every iteration or broadcasting from the client

assertions.description: Uses build-function form so workers load from file directly (not client-load-then-wrap)
assertions.expected: parallel.pool.Constant is called with a function handle (build function) that performs the load, e.g. parallel.pool.Constant(@() load(...)), NOT called with the loaded data variable like parallel.pool.Constant(data)

assertions.description: Uses .Value to access data inside parfor
assertions.expected: Inside parfor bodies, the loaded data is accessed via .Value property

assertions.description: Preserves the iterative optimization structure
assertions.expected: The function retains the for-loop-with-nested-parfor structure because each iteration depends on results from the previous iteration

evals.name: Extract loadlibrary from parfor body
evals.description: Refactor code that loads and unloads a shared library on every parfor iteration into a per-worker persistent pattern
evals.prompt: Can you make this code more efficient?

assertions.description: Uses parallel.pool.Constant for per-worker library loading
assertions.expected: The code uses parallel.pool.Constant to load the shared library once per worker, rather than calling loadlibrary on every iteration

assertions.description: Does not use spmd for library setup
assertions.expected: The code does not use spmd blocks for library loading — it uses parallel.pool.Constant with a build function instead

assertions.description: Provides a cleanup function that unloads the library
assertions.expected: parallel.pool.Constant is called with two function handle arguments: a build function that calls loadlibrary, and a cleanup function that calls unloadlibrary

assertions.description: Does not call loadlibrary inside the parfor body
assertions.expected: loadlibrary is called outside the parfor loop (e.g., in a build function passed to parallel.pool.Constant), not inside the parfor body

assertions.description: Does not call unloadlibrary inside the parfor body
assertions.expected: unloadlibrary is called outside the parfor loop (e.g., in a cleanup function passed to parallel.pool.Constant), not inside the parfor body

evals.name: Fix shared environment variable setup inside parfor
evals.description: Refactor code that calls setenv with the same values on every parfor iteration to use parpool EnvironmentVariables or parfevalOnAll
evals.prompt: Can you make this so we don't keep calling setenv in the loop? The workers still need those environment variables set because computeWithEngine reads them from the process environment internally.

assertions.description: Does not call setenv inside the parfor body
assertions.expected: setenv is not called inside the parfor loop — environment is configured before the loop via parpool EnvironmentVariables, parfevalOnAll, or equivalent one-time mechanism

assertions.description: Uses parpool EnvironmentVariables name-value pair to forward env vars to workers
assertions.expected: The pool is created with the EnvironmentVariables name-value argument so that environment variables are forwarded to workers at pool startup, rather than calling setenv on each iteration

assertions.description: Does not use spmd for environment setup
assertions.expected: The code does not use spmd blocks for environment variable setup — it uses the parpool EnvironmentVariables name-value argument instead

assertions.description: Preallocates output before parfor
assertions.expected: Output variable is preallocated (e.g., zeros) before the parfor loop

assertions.description: Retains client-side setenv calls before pool creation
assertions.expected: The client-side setenv calls (outside the parfor loop) are preserved so that the variable values exist in the client environment before EnvironmentVariables forwards them to workers

assertions.description: EnvironmentVariables receives only variable names, not values
assertions.expected: The EnvironmentVariables argument is a string array of variable names (e.g., ["LICENSE_SERVER", "CACHE_DIR"]), not a matrix of name-value pairs

assertions.description: Does not delete and recreate the pool to apply environment variables
assertions.expected: The code does not delete or shut down the pool and create a new one in order to apply environment variables. A trailing delete(pool) for end-of-function cleanup is acceptable, but creating a pool, deleting it, then creating another pool with different settings is not.

evals.name: Fix addpath/rmpath inside parfor
evals.description: Refactor code that calls addpath and rmpath on every parfor iteration to use parpool AdditionalPaths
evals.prompt: Can you make this code more efficient?

assertions.description: Uses parpool AdditionalPaths name-value pair to set worker paths
assertions.expected: The pool is created with the AdditionalPaths name-value argument so that required paths are available on workers at pool startup, rather than calling addpath on each iteration

assertions.description: Does not call addpath inside the parfor body
assertions.expected: addpath is not called inside the parfor loop — path is set up at pool creation time or via parfevalOnAll before the loop

assertions.description: Does not call rmpath inside the parfor body
assertions.expected: rmpath is not called inside the parfor loop — path lifecycle is managed by the pool

assertions.description: Does not use spmd for path setup
assertions.expected: The code does not use spmd blocks for path setup — it uses the parpool AdditionalPaths name-value argument instead

assertions.description: Does not delete and recreate the pool
assertions.expected: delete(pool

evals.name: Modernise spmd worker temp directory setup
evals.description: Refactor code that uses spmd to create and cd into temp directories before parfor into a modern pattern
evals.prompt: Can you improve this code? Each worker must have its own scratch directory.

assertions.description: Does not use spmd blocks
assertions.expected: The code does not use any spmd blocks — setup and cleanup are handled by parallel.pool.Constant

assertions.description: Uses parallel.pool.Constant with build and cleanup functions
assertions.expected: The code uses parallel.pool.Constant with a build function that creates (or obtains) a per-worker scratch directory, and a cleanup function that removes the directory. The build function may either cd into the directory or return its path for use via c.Value — both are valid.

assertions.description: Does not reference Composite variables or labindex inside parfor
assertions.expected: The code does not use labindex, spmdIndex, or any Composite indexing inside the parfor body

assertions.description: Cleanup is automatic, not in a trailing spmd block
assertions.expected: Directory cleanup is handled by the cleanup function of parallel.pool.Constant (runs when the Constant goes out of scope or pool shuts down), not by a manual rmdir in an spmd block
